title: Liber AL vel Legis
summary: The Book of the Law, the central revealed text of Thelema. It records the Cairo Working and frames the Aeon of Horus, True Will, Nuit, Hadit, and Aiwass.

title: Book 4 / Liber ABA
summary: Crowley's major instructional manual on yoga, magick, and the discipline of the magical life. It remains one of the portal's most useful bridges between theory and practice.

title: The Vision and the Voice
summary: Crowley's record of the thirty Enochian Aethyrs worked with Victor Neuburg. The text is central to his ideas about the Abyss, the Holy Guardian Angel, and the desert visionary current.

title: 777 and Other Qabalistic Writings
summary: A reference compendium of correspondences. It is indispensable for mapping Crowley's Qabalistic and ceremonial system, even though it is not itself a narrative work.

title: The Book of Thoth
summary: Crowley's tarot manual and commentary on the Thoth deck. It codifies many of his most visible tarot revisions, including the Tzaddi and Heh swap.

title: The Equinox, Vol. I, No. 1
summary: The first issue of Crowley's periodical for magical instruction, publications, rituals, and polemics. It is one of the chief print vehicles of his early system.

title: The Book of Lies
summary: A dense collection of short chapters, jokes, aphorisms, and symbolic reversals. The book rewards close reading and fits the portal's interest in compressed doctrine.

title: Liber Aleph vel CXI
summary: A letter-style work addressed to Crowley's magical son. It presents an intimate synthesis of ethics, spiritual instruction, and Crowley's worldview.

title: The Blue Equinox
summary: A later issue of The Equinox that collects manifestos, rituals, and doctrinal material from Crowley's post-war career in America.

title: Magick in Theory and Practice
summary: One of Crowley's most accessible explanations of how his magical theory is supposed to work in practice. It is a major source for modern readers.

title: The Confessions of Aleister Crowley
summary: Crowley's autobiography, written as a self-interpreting life story. Essential, but always to be read with the caution due an unreliable narrator.

title: Diary of a Drug Fiend
summary: Crowley's best-known novel, published under his own name. The book dramatizes addiction, discipline, and transformation through fiction.
